name: Named Data (NDN / content-addressed)
strengths: Same name asked twice = one network traversal. Routers cache automatically. Built-in multicast — popular content gets faster as it's accessed. Origin failure doesn't break access to already-published data.
tradeoffs: Per-recipient encryption fights caching (pick group encryption or no caching). Dynamic content needs synthetic names. Authentication is per-object, not per-channel — every object signed. Routing tables grow with name space.
real_world_examples: NDN (UCLA, named-data.net). CCN/CCNx. IPFS in spirit. BitTorrent. Production content distribution networks like Cloudflare implement subsets.
